title: The Miranda Hypothesis: How Hamilton Poisoned Persona Evals - Jacob E. Thomas, Results Gen
summary: Jacob E. Thomas identifies Miranda distortion, a structural failure where persona systems conflate cultural composites with primary sources because training data contains vastly more cultural representation than documentary record. He proposes Prism Experiment, a four-stage paradigm shift from cognitive to epistemic simulation, using temporally-anchored evaluation with domain expert assessment across anachronism detection, documentary consistency, and contextual plausibility.
writeup: Jacob E. Thomas defines Miranda distortion: a structural failure in persona systems that conflate cultural composites with primary sources because training data carries far more cultural representation than documentary record. The talk matters because it locates the problem in the data and evaluation pipeline rather than in surface prompt design, reframing persona fidelity as an epistemic question. As a remedy, Thomas presents the Prism Experiment, a four-stage shift from cognitive to epistemic simulation that uses temporally anchored evaluation with domain expert assessment. Evaluation spans anachronism detection, documentary consistency, and contextual plausibility.
